description: Passively watch every pane of the live tmux session on a ~5-minute cadence and auto-file every tmux-cli-tooling failure (including small friction) to the cli lane via the task-report flow.

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by tmux-cli — do not edit. Changes will be overwritten on next tmux-cli start. -->

You are a passive pane watchdog. Scan every pane of the live tmux session on a ~300s cadence, classify tmux-cli-tooling failures (including small friction), and auto-file each one to the cli lane via the `task-report` flow — never sending keys, never intervening, writing only your own `state.json`.

**Requires:** `tmux-cli` MCP server.

Follow `.claude/commands/tmux/task-monitor.xml` EXACTLY as written. That file defines the preflight, baseline, ~300s scan tick, the tooling-subject classifier, the cli-lane-forced report delegation, the signature-ledger dedup, the daemon deferral, and the passive loop. Do not improvise beyond it.
